canonical_concepts.label: neurons
section_paths: How are neurotransmitters stored and released?

canonical_concepts.label: neurotransmitters
definitions: Chemical substances produced by neurons that underlie behavior.

canonical_concepts.label: enzymes
definitions: Proteins that help build, breakdown or otherwise modify substances in our cells.
section_paths: How are neurotransmitters stored and released?

canonical_concepts.label: Blood-brain barrier (BBB)
definitions: A firewall-like structure that regulates the passage of nutrients into the brain.

canonical_concepts.label: Synaptic vesicles
definitions: very tiny spheres clustered at the presynaptic terminal with hollow centers into which approximately 10,000 molecules of a typical neurotransmitter can be stored for later release from a neuron.
section_paths: How are neurotransmitters stored and released?

canonical_concepts.label: Reuptake
definitions: The reabsorption of a neurotransmitter via a protein transporter located on the (pre-synaptic) membrane of the axon terminal.

canonical_concepts.label: synapse
definitions: The junction at which two neurons communicate via the release of a neurotransmitter molecule.
section_paths: How are neurotransmitters stored and released?

canonical_concepts.label: ionotropic receptors
definitions: transmembrane molecules that can 'open' or 'close' a channel that would allow smaller particles to travel in and out of the cell.
Not explicitly defined in this chunk.

canonical_concepts.label: metabotropic receptors
definitions: receptors linked to a G-protein that activate secondary messengers upon ligand binding.
Not explicitly defined in this chunk.

canonical_concepts.label: ligand-gated transmembrane ion channels
definitions: ionotropic receptors that open or close in response to ligand binding, allowing ions to travel through.

canonical_concepts.label: G-protein coupled receptors
definitions: a large group of evolutionarily related proteins that activate G-proteins and secondary messengers.

canonical_concepts.label: autoreceptor
definitions: A special type of protein receptor located on the membranes of nerve cells that is sensitive to the neurotransmitters released by the neuron on which it sits.
